title: Group By Key
code: const groupBy = <T>(arr: T[], key: (item: T) => string): Record<string, T[]> => arr.reduce((acc, item) => ({ ...acc, [key(item)]: [...(acc[key(item)] ?? []), item] }), {} as Record<string, T[]>)
description: Partitions an array into a map of arrays keyed by a selector function. The spread `{ ...acc, [k]: [...existing, item] }` creates a new object on each step — readable but **O(n²)** due to spreading. For large arrays, prefer a mutating `reduce` with `acc[k] ??= []`.
